name: PipelineTerraformPolicyCheck
description: Evaluate OPA or Sentinel policies from a directory or git repo against Terraform plan JSON
author: sethbacon

inputs.name: engine
inputs.label: Policy engine
inputs.helpMarkDown: Which policy engine to evaluate with. Install it first with the Pipeline policy agent installer task.

inputs.name: inputFile
inputs.label: Input file (plan JSON)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the JSON to evaluate, typically the output of 'terraform show -json <plan>' (e.g. $(TerraformShow.showFilePath)).

inputs.name: policyAgentPath
inputs.label: Policy agent path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional explicit path to the opa/sentinel binary. Defaults to the binary on the PATH.

inputs.name: policyPath
inputs.label: Policy directory
inputs.helpMarkDown: Directory containing the policies (e.g. from a multi-repo checkout).

inputs.name: policyRepoUrl
inputs.label: Policy repo URL
inputs.helpMarkDown: HTTPS git URL of the policy repository.

inputs.name: policyRepoRef
inputs.label: Ref (branch, tag, or SHA)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Branch, tag, or full 40-character commit SHA to check out. Pin a SHA for supply-chain integrity.

inputs.name: policyRepoSubdir
inputs.label: Subdirectory
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional subdirectory within the cloned repo that holds the policies.

inputs.name: policyRepoToken
inputs.label: Repo access token
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional token for a private repo. Pass a pipeline secret variable; it is injected via http.extraheader and never written to the clone URL or logs.

inputs.name: decisionPath
inputs.label: Decision path
inputs.helpMarkDown: OPA decision to evaluate (data.<path>), e.g. terraform/deny.

inputs.name: failMode
inputs.label: Fail mode
inputs.helpMarkDown: When to fail the task based on the decision result.

options.nonEmpty: Fail when the decision set/array is non-empty (deny convention)
options.defined: Fail when the decision is defined and truthy

inputs.name: defaultEnforcementLevel
inputs.label: Enforcement level
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enforcement level applied to generated policy config (ignored when a custom sentinel.hcl is supplied).

inputs.name: overrideSoftMandatory
inputs.label: Override soft-mandatory failures
inputs.helpMarkDown: When enabled, soft-mandatory policy failures warn but do not fail the task.

inputs.name: sentinelImportName
inputs.label: Static import name
inputs.helpMarkDown: Name of the static import that exposes the plan JSON to policies (import "<name>").

inputs.name: sentinelConfigPath
inputs.label: Custom sentinel.hcl path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. Use an existing sentinel.hcl instead of generating one. The task then gates purely on the exit code.

inputs.name: traceOutput
inputs.label: Trace output
inputs.helpMarkDown: Run sentinel apply with -trace for verbose debugging output.

inputs.name: publishTestResults
inputs.label: Publish results to Tests tab
inputs.helpMarkDown: Publish a JUnit report so policy outcomes appear in the pipeline Tests tab.

inputs.name: sarifOutput
inputs.label: Write a SARIF report
inputs.helpMarkDown: Write a SARIF 2.1.0 report of policy violations and expose its path via the `sarifFilePath` output variable. Publish it as a build artifact to surface findings in SARIF-aware tooling.

inputs.name: sarifPath
inputs.label: SARIF output path
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional explicit path for the SARIF report. When empty, a file is written to the agent temp directory; either way its path is exposed via the `sarifFilePath` output variable.

outputVariables.name: violationCount
outputVariables.description: The number of policy violations found.

outputVariables.name: resultsFilePath
outputVariables.description: Path to the raw engine output written to the agent temp directory.

outputVariables.name: sarifFilePath
outputVariables.description: Path to the SARIF 2.1.0 report (set only when 'Write a SARIF report' is enabled).

messages.InsecureUrlRejected: Insecure URL rejected: %s. Only HTTPS URLs are allowed.
